items.title: Crypto defies equity weakness as altcoin optimism builds into the weekend
items.description: The price of bitcoin hit $64,400, retesting a level it failed to penetrate on Monday, with a break higher opening the path toward the June 15 peak of $67,250.

items.title: Polymarket seeks approval to bring margin trading to U.S. customers
items.description: Polymarket's application to allow users to take positions that are not fully collateralized follows authorization granted to rival Kalshi in March.

items.title: Bitcoin's $60,000-$70,000 range becomes third most traded range in history
items.description: Bitcoin has spent 307 days in the $60,000-$70,000 range, the third longest consolidation in any $10,000 price band.

items.title: Metaplanet explores bringing bitcoin-backed digital credit to Japan
items.description: The bitcoin treasury company is working with JPYC and Progmat to explore tokenized credit products backed by bitcoin, aiming to create efficient, 24/7 credit markets in Japan.

items.title: Bitcoin gets a green light from a reliable momentum gauge. Here are key levels to watch
items.description: A smoother long-term MACD just flipped bullish, pointing to more upside. Here are key levels that will decide if the ongoing bounce turns into a full blown uptrend.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin tops $64,000 as selling pressure on Coinbase eases
items.description: Spot bitcoin funds lost about $95 million on Thursday and ether funds roughly $52 million, ending the one bright spot in crypto's institutional flows even as prices rallied.

items.title: XRP up 2% as buyers push through $1.10 resistance
items.description: A late-session volume surge carried XRP above its recent range, with traders now watching whether $1.10 can hold as support.

items.title: Bitcoin's quiet split: Strong in USD, lagging in JPY as Yen rises on intervention fears
items.description: A sharp rise in the yen has left bitcoin and other major cryptocurrencies underperforming in yen terms compared with their dollar-based trading pairs.

items.title: TeraWulf eyes $3.5B debt raise for Anthropic-linked data center: Report
items.description: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf is reportedly seeking $3.5 billion in debt financing led by Morgan Stanley for its Kentucky data center campus leased by AI company Anthropic.

items.title: Metaplanet explores Bitcoin-backed digital credit with JPYC in Japan
items.description: Metaplanet said it is studying Bitcoin-backed digital credit products in Japan with JPYC and Progmat, but no product has been launched.

items.title: Bitcoin returns to $64.3K with new three-week BTC price highs imminent
items.description: Bitcoin bulls faced "crucial resistance" at $65,000 next as BTC price action diverged from oil and US dollar strength.

items.title: EU parliament passes ‘chat control,’ allowing private chat scans until 2028
items.description: EU lawmakers have again allowed tech firms to scan chats for abuse material, but exempted end-to-end encrypted messages.

items.title: Robinhood Chain sees over $70M in ETH bridged during first week
items.description: Robinhood’s choice to use Ethereum further solidifies its position as the “ultimate settlement layer and liquidity foundation for tokenized assets,” said HashKey Group’s Tim Sun.

items.title: Zcash targets July 28 launch for Ironwood network upgrade
items.description: Zcash’s next upgrade will replace the once-compromised Orchard pool and may offer clues about whether counterfeit tokens were created via a recent bug.

items.title: Hackers tried to backdoor Injective npm package to steal wallet keys
items.description: The incident is significant for developers and applications that handle Injective wallet workflows, Socket researchers said.

items.title: DeFi may be ‘quietly re-rating’ given outperformance against Bitcoin: Bitwise
items.description: “DeFi usually swings much harder than Bitcoin, so holding up this well is unusual,” says Bitwise, as DeFi tokens have outperformed Bitcoin.

items.title: Meet SK Hynix, the trillion-dollar South Korean chipmaker debuting on U.S. markets
items.description: Following a more than sevenfold rally in its stock price over the past year, South Korea's SK Hynix is listing on the Nasdaq.

items.title: Delta expects higher airfare to last, bringing 2026 profit goal in reach, CEO says
items.description: Delta is the first of the U.S. airlines to report second-quarter results.

items.title: Meta found to breach EU laws with 'addictive' Instagram, Facebook designs
items.description: Meta is an apparent breach of EU laws due to addictive design features on Instagram and Facebook, according to preliminary findings on Friday.

items.title: AI, data center fears could be key in closely watched Michigan Democratic Senate primary
items.description: Abdul El-Sayed and Rep. Haley Stevens will face off in the Michigan Democratic Senate primary on Aug. 4.

items.title: World oil demand set for first annual decline since 2020, IEA says
items.description: Renewed escalation in the U.S.-Iran war could complicate matters and further cloud the outlook, the IEA warned Friday.

items.title: OpenAI exec Fidji Simo says she's stepping down due to chronic illness, will transition to advisor
items.description: Fidji Simo stepped away from OpenAI in April for a medical leave, says she now has to focus on recovery.

items.title: New Fed task force members share Chairman Kevin Warsh's embrace of AI
items.description: The three members of a task force advising the Fed on artificial intelligence are strong proponents of the technology.

items.title: U.S. to continue 'technical talks' with Iran after Trump said ceasefire was 'over'
items.description: The ceasefire signed last month came under serious strain in recent days as the U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged attacks for consecutive days this week.

items.title: The luxury square that shows London’s mansions are not selling
items.description: In Knightsbridge’s Montpelier Square, property prices have tumbled from a decade ago as foreign buyers look elsewhere

items.title: Why AI could be a financial ‘sludge’ buster

items.title: Apollo trumps Castlelake with £5.7bn deal to buy easyJet

items.title: EasyJet: orange upstart that changed flying prepares to go private
items.description: Pivotal moment for Europe’s low-cost pioneer as US suitors circle

items.title: UBS helped trigger exodus from Blue Owl private credit fund
items.description: Bank advised some clients to cut exposure, hitting a fund it had been instrumental in setting up

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z1Axn8M5Fcs! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: As SK Hynix lands in the U.S., here’s why investors should be wary of the chip sector’s ‘bubble-like’ volatility
items.description: Traders may hope South Korea-type turbulence won’t be imported into Wall Street

items.title: Delta reports record revenue and a profit beat, even as fuel costs surge
items.description: Delta Air Lines’ stock was set to gain despite the company saying that profit fell — but beat expectations — even after the air carrier absorbed the highest quarterly fuel expense in its history.

items.title: Stocks are seeing increasingly wild swings post-earnings. Why even good numbers often aren’t enough to impress.
items.description: The second-quarter earnings season is off to a volatile start, offering an early test of whether investors’ high expectations for corporate profits can hold up.

items.title: ‘I’ve no idea how they got her number’: My student-loan servicer called a friend after I missed a payment. Is that legal?
items.description: “She said it’s the second message they have left.”

items.title: 1,500 Americans filed for bankruptcy each day last year. They should have done this instead.
items.description: Personal bankruptcy filings surged 47% between 2022 and 2025. Here’s what you should do before deciding to file.

items.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
items.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”

items.title: As AI spending concerns grow, here’s the bet to make ahead of critical stress test, says veteran Jefferies strategist
items.description: Pick and shovel stocks benefit from hyperscaler capex upfront, while those spending the money must wait years to find out if there will be a return on their investments

items.title: My insurance company said my roof lost a few tiles. Loss adjusters found $10,000 in storm damage. How could this happen?
items.description: “My house shook violently from the wind.”

2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: At least 12 people die in Spanish wildfire as heatwave grips Europe
items.description: Alarm over summer of blazes as third wave of extreme heat hits region

items.title: FirstFT: SK Hynix completes biggest US IPO by a foreign company
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Mervyn King picked for Fed reform and Spanish forest fires kill 12

items.title: Have you joined the tartan army?
items.description: 21 ways to find your clan

items.title: EU warns Meta over Facebook and Instagram’s addictive feeds
items.description: Brussels says tech group has failed to adequately assess and mitigate risks that could have a negative impact on users

items.title: IEA warns of petrol and diesel supply crunch
items.description: Refineries in Gulf and Russia hit by impact of wars, while global consumption has remained high

items.title: Japan sheds ties as price index shake-up reflects changing habits
items.description: Revision of inflation ‘basket’ ditches office wear in favour of protein powder and pet insurance

items.title: Bayer sells stake in contraceptives business to Apollo in €3bn financing deal
items.description: Sale will help German pharmaceuticals group to bolster its balance sheet after years of litigation over Roundup weedkiller

items.title: Skills are shaping up to be Burnham’s next crusade
items.description: Whether it’s good public transport or young people’s futures, he knows where to make government matter to voters

items.title: Meta Ordered by E.U. to Alter ‘Addictive Design’ of Instagram and Facebook
items.description: European Union authorities said the company’s use of “addictive design” violated a digital safety law.

items.title: You’re Freezing in Your Office Building. Here’s Why.
items.description: Office temperatures are typically set in the low 70s during summer. But where you sit and the efficiency of the cooling system can make you feel colder.

items.title: Hotel Workers Hoped the World Cup Would Boost Their Hours. It Didn’t.
items.description: Even as matches have drawn large crowds, hotel bookings have lagged. And workers in host cities say they’re struggling.

items.title: The Message From Barcelona’s New Sustainable Tourism Leader: ‘Not One Tourist More.’
items.description: As the commissioner for sustainable tourism, José Antonio Donaire’s job is to diminish the impact of the nearly 16 million annual visitors to the Catalan capital. Here’s what he’s planning.

items.title: Oil Prices Wobble As a Week of Hostilities Derails Shipping
items.description: The latest turmoil, sparked by attacks on three commercial ships in the strait days ago, pushed the price of crude back above prewar levels.

items.title: John Deere Farm Equipment Owners Have Right-to-Repair, F.T.C. Says
items.description: A settlement by the company with the Federal Trade Commission will allow farmers and local mechanics to make their own fixes, instead of relying on authorized dealers.

items.title: Delta, United and Other Airlines Are Offering Lower-Cost Business Class Options: What to Know
items.description: Delta is the latest U.S. airline to introduce stripped down, luxury-class fares. What do you get for your money, and is it worth it? What you need to know.

items.title: Volkswagen Is Cutting Production as Sales in China Plunge
items.description: The German automaker has struggled to compete with fast-growing Chinese companies that offer more affordable and sophisticated electric vehicles.
